prompt: A newly certified LEED BD+C office building records first-year metered energy use 15% above the design energy model. With respect to the awarded BD+C certification itself, what is required?

options.text: Submit a LEED interpretation request to justify the higher consumption
Implement energy conservation measures to reduce consumption to modeled levels
No action is required because BD+C certification is based on design and construction compliance, not ongoing metered performance
Recalibrate the energy model to match actual consumption for future reference

explanation: LEED BD+C certification is conferred based on design and construction documentation and modeled compliance at certification. Post-occupancy energy variance does not by itself revoke BD+C certification, although the owner may still pursue operational improvements voluntarily.

distractorExplanations.a: Interpretations clarify credit requirements during certification; they are not the mechanism for addressing post-occupancy performance variance.
distractorExplanations.b: Energy conservation may be good practice, but it is not required to maintain an already awarded BD+C certification.
distractorExplanations.d: Recalibrating the model may inform future operations but is not required to preserve BD+C certification status.
